note: This manifest describes the oil-project export package. All downstream consumers should validate against these schemas before rendering data.

intelligence.json.description: Live export — OilExport envelope with hormuz_risk, energy_events[], commodity_signals[]. Generated by `npm run export`. This is the primary file consumed by the oil frontend.
intelligence.json.consumer_notes: Poll this file for live data. The hormuz_risk.risk_level field drives the Hormuz risk indicator. energy_events[] is sorted by descending escalation_potential.

oil-events.json.description: Sample validated export — 14 representative oil-related events with full coordinate and location metadata. Used by the oil project team to validate frontend rendering of new fields (lat, lng, coordinate_quality, location_type, related_asset).
oil-events.json.consumer_notes: Not a live feed. Use for frontend integration testing and schema validation only. Replace with intelligence.json in production.

energy-indicators.json.description: Sample energy indicator time series — crude prices (Brent, WTI, Henry Hub), production volumes (global, OPEC, US, Saudi Arabia, Iraq), inventory levels, chokepoint transit volumes, spare capacity, and proved reserves.
energy-indicators.json.consumer_notes: Not a live feed. Use for frontend chart integration testing. Live indicators are served via energy-indicators.json when the EIA connector is enabled.

OilEvent.description: A single energy-related intelligence event enriched with coordinate metadata.

fields.event_id: string — sha256 deterministic ID, 24 chars. Stable across pipeline runs.
fields.storyline_id: string? — links to a cross-day narrative thread. Absent for emerging events.
fields.title: string — wire-service style headline, max 180 chars.
fields.summary: string — 2–4 sentence factual summary, max 500 chars.
fields.event_type: string — one of the IntelligenceEvent event type enum values.
fields.severity: number — 1 (minimal) to 5 (critical). Scale: 5=multiple countries/global markets affected.
fields.countries: string[] — ISO 3166-1 alpha-3. Primary country first.
fields.escalation_potential: number — 0.0–1.0. Probability this event escalates in severity within 7 days.
fields.market_relevance: number — 0.0–1.0. Oil market price-impact signal strength.
fields.is_supply_disruption: boolean — true when the event directly reduces available supply.
fields.is_hormuz_related: boolean — true when the event directly affects Hormuz transit risk.
fields.first_seen_at: string — ISO 8601 datetime of first pipeline detection.
fields.source_ids: string[] — RSS source identifiers that contributed to this event.
fields.lat: number? — WGS-84 latitude. Absent when coordinate_quality is 'missing'.
fields.lng: number? — WGS-84 longitude. Absent when coordinate_quality is 'missing'.
fields.coordinate_quality: enum? — 'source_exact' | 'source_approx' | 'country_centroid' | 'missing'. Always check this before rendering a map marker.
fields.related_asset: string? — primary commodity or infrastructure type: 'oil' | 'gas' | 'chokepoint' | 'infrastructure'.

CoordinateQuality.description: Enum that describes how precisely the lat/lng coordinates locate the event. Set by the Data Hub — never inferred by the frontend.

values.source_exact: Confirmed GPS or precise address from source data (e.g. ACLED field report with verified coordinates).
values.source_approx: Geocoded city or district from source data (e.g. AI-extracted location name resolved to centroid).
values.country_centroid: Geographic centroid of the primary country. Used when no sub-national location is known. Sufficient for country-level map markers, insufficient for precise event location claims.
values.missing: No coordinates available. Event should not be placed on a precise map marker; use country outline or omit from map entirely.

CoordinateQuality.frontend_guidance: Render source_exact and source_approx as precise markers. Render country_centroid as a country-level indicator (different icon/opacity). Never render missing events as map markers.

OilLocationType.description: Enum that characterizes the type of geographic location associated with the event.

values.point: A precise GPS point, such as a specific facility or installation.
values.city: City-level granularity (e.g. Basra, Kuwait City, Abadan).
values.region: Sub-national region or province (e.g. Khuzestan Province, Eastern Province).
values.country: Country-level only — no sub-national location identified.
values.chokepoint: A named maritime chokepoint (Strait of Hormuz, Bab-el-Mandeb, Strait of Malacca, Suez Canal).
values.infrastructure: Oil/gas infrastructure (pipeline, export terminal, refinery, offshore platform, LNG facility).
values.offshore: An offshore area without a precise location (e.g. 'waters off Nigeria').
values.unknown: Location type could not be determined.

EnergyIndicator.description: A named energy metric with a time series of values.

fields.indicator_key: string — machine-readable key, e.g. 'brent_crude_usd_bbl'. Stable across versions.
fields.unit: string — measurement unit, e.g. 'USD/barrel', 'million barrels per day'.
fields.series: array — time series, most recent first. Each entry: { period: 'YYYY-MM-DD', value: number }.

consumer_contract.version_gating: Consumers MUST check schema_version before parsing. Reject any file where schema_version major version differs from expected.
consumer_contract.coordinate_rendering: Consumers MUST check coordinate_quality before rendering map markers. Never assume lat/lng precision from the values alone.
consumer_contract.supply_disruption_flag: is_supply_disruption is set deterministically by the Data Hub exporter — do not recompute on the frontend.
consumer_contract.hormuz_risk: The hormuz_risk object in intelligence.json is the canonical Hormuz risk signal. Do not derive risk level from event list alone.
consumer_contract.data_freshness: intelligence.json is regenerated daily. energy-indicators.json is updated when the EIA connector runs (currently weekly). oil-events.json is a static sample.
consumer_contract.schema_stability: Fields are never renamed or removed within a schema_version. New optional fields may be added in minor releases. Breaking changes bump schema_version.
